confirmed.title: Gate-id correlation guard ineffective when resume_run() called without gate_id parameter
confirmed.verification: Verified in /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-grc/src/graph.py:185-216 and /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-grc/src/nodes/gates.py:140. The resume_run() function has an optional gate_id parameter (defaults to None, line 186). When called without this parameter, line 213-214 condition fails (gate_id is None), so "gate_id" is NOT added to resume_value. The gate-correlation guard at gates.py:140 checks: `resp.get("gate_id") not in (None, gate_id)`. When gate_id is missing from the response, resp.get("gate_id") returns None. The check becomes `None not in (None, current_gate_id)` which is False, so the guard does NOT re-prompt and accepts the response unconditionally. This is confirmed as a known documented issue: the docstring at graph.py:198-200 explicitly states "Without it the guard is unreachable." Test evidence: test_resume_run_approves_gate_1 (line 128-137) and test_resume_run_recovers_from_malformed_decision (line 140-156) both call resume_run() without gate_id and pass, but only test gate_1. The correlation test test_gate_id_correlation_rejects_mismatched_decision (line 75-89) DOES pass gate_id and verifies the guard works when gate_id is provided. The vulnerability is reachable in a multi-gate scenario (gate_1 → gate_2) where someone calls resume_run() without gate_id on a run parked at gate_2—the guard would be ineffective. This is not in the WONTFIX list (pass 7 rationale in HARDENING_LOG line 288-302).
confirmed.recommended_action: Make gate_id a required parameter in resume_run() by removing the default value (change line 186 from `gate_id: str | None = None` to `gate_id: str`). All call sites must explicitly pass the gate ID. Update the docstring to reflect this is mandatory. Add a test that calls resume_run() without gate_id to catch regression if the default is accidentally restored. Alternatively, implement auto-detection of which gate is currently parked and validate correlation automatically without requiring the caller to pass it—but this is more complex and the simple fix (required parameter) is preferred.
confirmed.proposed_fix: Make gate_id a required parameter in resume_run() by removing the default value (change 'gate_id: str | None = None' to 'gate_id: str'). All call sites (currently only in tests) must explicitly pass the gate ID. Alternatively, implement auto-detection of which gate is currently parked and validate correlation automatically without requiring the caller to pass it.

confirmed.title: Missing [tool.setuptools] configuration breaks package discovery in src-layout build
confirmed.verification: Read /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-grc/pyproject.toml (lines 1-56): No [tool.setuptools] section exists. Verified actual project structure: src/ contains __init__.py + subpackages (agents/, infrastructure/, nodes/), plus modules (checkpointer.py, graph.py, reasoning_client.py, state.py). Examined dist-info top_level.txt from current editable install (/Users/[REDACTED]/hc-grc/.venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/hc_grc-0.1.0.dist-info/top_level.txt): Lists [__init__, agents, checkpointer, graph, infrastructure, nodes, reasoning_client, state], proving setuptools flattened src/ contents as top-level. Checked import patterns in src/agents/data_acquisition/agent.py: Uses relative imports like `from ...state import HCGRCState`, which depend on src/ being on sys.path. Verified .pth file (/Users/[REDACTED]/hc-grc/.venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/__editable__.hc_grc-0.1.0.pth) contains `/Users/[REDACTED]/hc-grc/src`, masking the defect in editable mode. Confirmed tests use `from src.agents` imports that only work because conftest.py (line 17) adds repo root to sys.path. The defect is HIDDEN in development but would cause ImportError in a normal wheel install due to broken relative import resolution.
confirmed.recommended_action: Add explicit [tool.setuptools] configuration to pyproject.toml to fix src-layout discovery. The minimum fix is: [tool.setuptools] / package-dir = { "" = "src" } / [tool.setuptools.find] / where = ["src"]. This ensures setuptools correctly discovers agents/, infrastructure/, nodes/ as site-packages/agents/, site-packages/infrastructure/, site-packages/nodes/ (not site-packages/src/*). Also exclude tests from wheel by adding exclude = ["tests"] to [tool.setuptools.find] section. This addresses both the package-dir ambiguity AND prevents test code from shipping in distributions.
confirmed.proposed_fix:
Add explicit [tool.setuptools] configuration to pyproject.toml:

[tool.setuptools]
package-dir = { "" = "src" }
packages = ["agents", "infrastructure", "nodes"]

Alternatively, use dynamic discovery with proper exclusion:

[tool.setuptools]
package-dir = { "" = "src" }

[tool.setuptools.find]
where = ["src"]

And exclude tests from the distribution (currently included inadvertently).
